# The Upper Colorado Corridor

> Spillover Predictor's first end-to-end corridor. Within a hydrologically connected
> corridor we name which parcels are structurally load-bearing for specific downstream
> beneficiaries, with calibrated uncertainty, and we tested those ecological scores
> against real water-quality data and reported, in public, that they do not yet beat a
> two-variable baseline. This page is a data-driven landing built entirely from the
> upper_colorado_corridor_mvp run; every number resolves to data/bundle.js (window.APP_DATA).

engine: spillover_predictor_v3.0
model_card: keystone_state_space_v0_1
case_id: upper_colorado_corridor_mvp
artifact: v1.7-agent-portal
data_mode: public_live
prereg: SHA-256 frozen 2026-05-28, before unblinding
seed: 20260507

## Why this exists (the market motivation)

The world needs to move roughly $700B/yr into nature (Paulson/TNC biodiversity finance gap)
and >50% of global GDP is nature-dependent (WEF/PwC), yet capital cannot underwrite a diffuse
public good ("healthier watersheds in general"); it can only price a specific asset tied to a
specific beneficiary cashflow at risk. Site-level scores measure condition, not dependency.
An ecosystem is a network: keystones are high-centrality nodes, spillovers are edge flows, and
a beneficiary is where a flow becomes a cashflow. Resolving that source->conveyance->demand
dependency graph is the missing translation layer between what ecology can observe and what a
market can price. The credibility bar is set by carbon's collapse (independent review found the
large majority of a leading standard's REDD+ credits delivered no real benefit; nature-based
prices fell from ~$15 to ~$1), so a pre-registered, concurrent-validity-tested, uncertainty-honest
engine is the flight-to-quality asset. (These $ figures are EXTERNAL market context, not the
corridor's own economics.)

## The primitive: the keystone polygon

The atomic unit is the "keystone polygon": a geographically delineated ecological asset (a land
polygon) whose spatial position and mechanistic pathways exert disproportionate influence over the
stability and productivity of the surrounding managed estate and its downstream beneficiaries. Named
and scored one polygon at a time, not a portfolio average or a site rating. Scientific lineage
(species -> structure -> polygon): keystone species and structural (not abundance) influence
(Paine 1966); keystone STRUCTURES, place-as-keystone (Tews et al. 2004); cross-boundary meta-ecosystem
flows and ecosystem-service "servicesheds" (Loreau et al. 2003; Bagstad et al. 2013); graph centrality
of habitat patches as a measurable spatial keystone (Urban & Keitt 2001); spatial non-stationarity
licensing per-polygon underwriting (GWR, Brunsdon et al. 1996). Naming the land polygon as the
underwriting unit is the new synthesis (Gutierrez, "Naturalizing Finance: The Land Next Door").

## Dual-legible by design (the agentic economy)

The deliverable ships human-readable AND agent-consumable: this llms.txt plus machine-readable,
provenance-carrying data, so an AI agent pre-screening a deal can re-pull each source and re-run each
check itself rather than discount a document it cannot parse. Every figure traces to a source.

## Honesty status (read this first)

- Grade: SCREENING-GRADE, not investment-grade. Proves the engine, not investability lift.
- The headline result is NEGATIVE and is the hero of the page, not a footnote.
- No corridor-level dollar-at-risk or cap rate is computed anywhere; none is shown. The only
  dollar figures on the page are external market-context facts (the $700B gap, carbon prices).
  The ask names the missing corridor dollar as the next step.
- Parcels are candidate envelopes: 24 verified_boundary, 32 public_seed_envelope of 56.
- Mechanism evidence is modeled-overlay (336 records; live_comid_count = 0).
- No mechanism clears validated_incremental.

## The claim, and its boundary

allowed: evidence navigation, screening-grade diligence, verification routing.
do_not_infer:
  - parcel-caused facility outcomes (routing is service-area, not causal).
  - treatment effects from causal-readiness tiers.
  - station proximity as mechanism verification.
  - screening outputs as investment-performance evidence.
caveats:
  - Homestake beneficiaries are Aurora Water and Colorado Springs Utilities, not Denver Water.
  - Ute Water primary supply is Plateau Creek / Grand Mesa; the Colorado is backup.
  - Moab Regional Hospital is on municipal wells, not a direct Colorado River intake.

## The corridor

length_km: 230
bbox: [-109.75, 38.20, -106.05, 39.95]
catchments: 182   huc8_subbasins: 20   parcels: 56   zones: 3
zones:
  - source_headwaters      (9,000 to 12,000 ft) generate the water
  - connector_mainstem     (5,500 to 7,500 ft)  convey it
  - demand_valley_canyon   (4,500 to 5,500 ft)  consume it
beneficiaries: 16, routed by hydrology through zone-weighted service profiles.

## The graph result (forward win, setup for the reckoning)

forward_impact_paths: 255
reverse_dependency_paths: 199
matched_both_ways: 44 of 56
keystone_ratio: 4 of 56 (7.1%) clear the prior credible interval (ci_low > 0.5), all Eagle
            headwaters (Red Cliff / Eagle River); 16 of 56 (28.6%) at keystone_score >= 0.50.
top_parcel: ucc-red_cliff_eagle-03, keystone posterior mean 0.67 (90% CI 0.55 to 0.79), zone
            source_headwaters, provenance verified_boundary (Eagle County parcels portal, live),
            rank 1 of 56. Clears the prior; BELOW the keystone gate (0.72) and investable gate (0.80).
gates: keystone 0.72, investable 0.80, bad_neighbor 0.55.

## The reckoning (concurrent validity vs 232 real stations)

method: COMID-catchment unit; leave-one-HUC8-out spatial CV; 2000-permutation null;
        macro-gradient control = elevation + % developed.
in_sample_signal_is_real:
  turbidity   n=45  rho=-0.329 p=0.026
  ssc         n=38  rho=-0.373 p=0.021
  tss         n=63  rho=-0.424 p=0.001
  conductance n=140 rho=-0.478 p=0.0005
but:
  forest_pct_alone_rho=-0.391 beats the composite (-0.329)
  partial rho after elevation+developed control: -0.047 (signal collapses)
out_of_sample_r2 (leave-one-HUC8-out, log-turbidity, n=45; higher is better):
  elevation + %developed only .... +0.403   (baseline WINS)
  forest % only .................. -0.375
  hand-set sediment_avoidance .... -0.398
  all mechanism features (refit) . -1.017
  mechanism features + confounders +0.270  (dR2 -0.13 vs baseline)
verdict: associated_not_incremental (WEAK)
diagnosis: the bottleneck is the FEATURES, not the weights; their relationship to water
           quality is spatially non-stationary and does not transfer to a new sub-basin.
second_mechanism: hydrology_retention vs baseflow index -> not_supported (wrong sign;
           forest-based retention peaks in flashier high-elevation snowmelt headwaters).

## The recalibration (the honest turn), computed live

The interactive recomputes the posterior LIVE from the engine's real precision-weighted Gaussian
update in logit space: obs_sd = MAX_SD -
(MAX_SD-BASE_SD)*sqrt(clip(oof_r2,0,1)), BASE_SD 0.45, MAX_SD 1.40, prior_p 0.54, prior_sd 0.82,
mechanism_value 0.70. As observation noise widens from the synthetic (overconfident) obs_sd 0.678
to the skill-calibrated obs_sd 1.40 (sediment_avoidance OOF R2 ~ 0.0), the posterior mean slides
from a falsely-confident 0.638 to 0.583, back toward the mechanism prior of 0.54. Widening the
noise pulls the posterior mean monotonically toward the prior mean; it cannot manufacture
confidence the data does not support. (Note: the mechanism prior here is 0.54; the parcel-level
keystone posterior uses a Beta(2,2) prior, mean 0.5 -- two different priors.)

## The ask

one real parcel, one verified payor, one dollar.
contact: corridor@graphoflife.com

## Provenance of this page

source_run: upper_colorado_corridor_mvp (private engine run)
geometry: data/geo.js (parcels, 232 stations, arcs, zone hulls, OOF scatter arrays), emitted from
          the run's processed GeoJSON and its frozen validity record.
hydrography: data/hydro.js (the corridor's real river network, HUC-8 basin frame, reservoirs),
          built from USGS WBD + NHDPlus HR (ArcGIS REST): dissolved by river name, clipped to the
          Colorado corridor HUC-8s, irrigation infrastructure dropped. Basemap: MapLibre GL + CARTO
          dark-matter + Terrarium DEM hillshade, all degrading to a solid dark canvas / d3 SVG
          fallback offline.
numbers: data/bundle.js (window.APP_DATA). No value is invented; where a corridor value does not
         exist (a dollar-at-risk / cap rate), the page says so. External market figures (the
         $700B gap, carbon prices) are sourced context, clearly distinct from corridor economics.
oof_report: a pre-registered, frozen leave-one-HUC8-out evidence record (private).
